entries.text: hi guys this is part two to my first
navigating friendships abroad video a
quick PSA before we begin I'm making
this video with the main reason of
letting people who are moving to a
foreign country know what to expect when
making friendships and navigating those
friendships specifically in Korea
because of course that's where my
experience is I've often been asked
whether you need need to learn Korean
when moving to Korea and my answer to
that is yes you definitely do need to
learn Korean before you live here and I
will explain why that is so I've seen a
lot of foreigners saying that you don't
need Korean to live in Korea especially
if you live in Soul which I don't really
agree with yes you can get by if you
don't know Korean and you live in Soul
because a lot of the time staff in
restaurants and cafes do speak English
so you are able to navigate you know
ordering food and drinks and such like
that but in terms of having a good
quality of life here as well as making
friends here you definitely do need to
learn Korean and so this is obviously
what I would advise you do because you
are going to have a much better time
here if you at least speak basic Korean
also honestly it's just polite you know
you're moving to another country so I
think it's just shows respect of that
country if you at least put effort into
learning the native language trust me I
understand that learning foreign
languages can be very challenging I've
struggled a lot with learning Korean it
has been harder for me than other people
possibly because I know some people pick
up foreign languages really easily for
me I do not I have to really study quite
hard before I even came to Korea I
learned very very very basic Korean I
learned how to say hello please and
thank you so that I could at least show
from the very start that I was putting
effort into speaking their language and
I will say that Korean people will
respect that you at least put effort in
even if your Korean is not perfect they
definitely just appreciate the attempt
but this video is about friendships in
Korea and in order to make friendships
and form connections with people as you
can imagine knowing the native language
is very important and as I mentioned
before I've seen many foreigners
speaking about how you don't need Korean
in order to do practical things here in
Korea like ordering food and stuff like
that and whilst that is true I don't
really see many foreigners speaking
about the social aspect of living here
in Korea and how much more challenging
that side of living here is if you don't
speak Korean to kind of tell you how
important this really is when I first
came to Korea with the Epic program I
was not placed in Soul and a lot of
people aren't because as you can imagine
soul is the number one place that
everyone puts down for their preferred
placement and so the competition is
incredibly hi so I was one of the people
who I put Soul down as my preferred
location but I got placed in Kongo and
the area I was placed in was very rural
and hardly anybody spoke English
whatsoever take that back nobody around
me spoke English
so yeah look here comes a consequence
consequence consequence consequences of
my actions chasing me right now as you
can imagine that was not a fun time for
me I used to just sit there while my
co-workers would be chatting away not
really having a clue what was going on
and obviously not being able to join in
conversations I often would feel left
out and that can definitely lead to a
feeling of isolation over time but you
know it did have the pro of it made me
even more determined to learn Korean and
learn Korean very quickly so that I
could you know include myself in
conversations and I didn't have to just
be the Foreigner that couldn't really
understand what was going on I would say
that learning Korean is actually a
necessity if you're coming to live here
especially if you don't want that
feeling of isolation and being the odd
one out which I think nobody really
would want that feeling so I definitely
recommend that you learn Korean because
the social aspect of living in Korea
when you don't understand the language
is obviously going to be very very
challenging it was for me and it was for
other friends that I had here who also
didn't know much Korean before moving
here honestly if I didn't have other
foreign friends in Korea during the
first month that I was here I would have
felt very very lonely indeed because I
just couldn't engage with the Korean
people around me but like I mentioned it
did have the pro of me being incredibly
motivated to learn Korean as quickly as
possible so once my Korean got to a
decent enough level I did make a couple
of Korean friends here I made friends
with a really sweet girl who worked at
my school with me and we would go out on
coffee dates and lunch dates and stuff
like that and have just very basic
conversation because my career at the
time although it had improved very quick
quickly it was still very much beginner
level so our conversations would be very
basic we would communicate half in
Korean and half in English a lot of the
time I would say that learning Korean is
90% of forming friendships here and then
the other 10% comes from culture even if
you are fluent in Korean there are
obviously still going to be cultural
differences between you and the people
of the country that you're living in
again before I even set foot in Korea
for the first time I did learn very
basic Korean but I also learned a lot
about Korean culture I did a lot of
research on especially what not to do
here um I didn't want to be you know the
Foreigner who came here and just
offended everybody that they met so I
did a lot of research on how not to
offend Korean people but obviously
knowing a culture and respecting a
culture doesn't mean that you and your
mannerisms fit into that culture
especially initially I had spent 22
years of my life in the UK before moving
here and the UK needless to say is a
very very different culture to Korean
culture and obviously having grown up in
a completely different culture that does
come across when you're forming
friendships here it can sometimes feel
like even though there is no language
barrier there is a culture barrier and
even though I made some really amazing
friends here I did still feel closer to
my foreign friends that were living in
Korea at the time because I well two of
them were also British so uh they had
very very similar experiences to me and
had grown up in very similar
environments to me so on a cultural
level I felt much closer to my foreign
friends here as a result of that I would
say I definitely felt a closer
connection with my foreign friends as
opposed to the Korean friends that I
made I say that with absolutely no
disrespect meant towards my Korean
friends whatsoever because they are some
of the sweetest most caring people I
have ever met in my entire life but at
that time especially I just felt a much
deeper connection with the foreign
friends that I had made here because I
felt like I could just relate to them
more another thing that I think is worth
mentioning is that I found making
friends with the opposite gender much
more challenging here in Korea than in
the UK I didn't know that this was a
thing until I experienced it but
basically I made friends with a guy who
again I had met through work there was
nothing there on either side he actually
had told me previously that he only
dated Korean girls I didn't see him like
that either so completely PL platonic
platonic completely PL
platonic and you know we wouldn't see
each other that often outside of school
we would get coffee together sometimes
and we would also go to uh PC rooms
together and then all of a sudden one
day he just started completely ignoring
me and I thought I had done something
wrong because he just acted as if
honestly as if I just wasn't there at
all he just began to act like I was
invisible and I was like oh I have no
idea what I've done but I guess I really
offended him somehow and so one day I
approached him and was like Hey if I did
something to upset you I'm really sorry
but let me know what I did wrong so that
you know I can apologize for that and
hopefully we can like still remain
friends and he was like no sorry I have
a girlfriend
now and I was like okay I'm happy for
you but what does that have to do with
our friendship I don't understand and he
was like she told me she's not not happy
with me having friends of the opposite
gender so we can't be friends
anymore I was really disappointed
because I lost a friend who was into
gaming honestly when he told me that his
girlfriend didn't allow him to have
friends of the opposite gender that's a
huge red flag in my opinion so I went
out with my other Korean friend the girl
who I mentioned earlier in the video who
we used to go out and get coffee and
we'd go on lunch days together that
friend I met up with her to rant about
this and I was saying you know that's
absolutely crazy like I can't believe
that his other half told him like he
can't have friends of the opposite
gender so he just like dropped all of
the friends that he had who were females
like that's crazy and she was just like
no that's that's pretty normal and I was
stunned because I'm not going to say it
never happens in the UK because it
definitely does but I think in the UK
most people would agree that if somebody
says you can't have friends that are the
opposite gender to you it's seen as a
red flag it's seen as kind of
controlling so for her to turn around
and tell me that that was a completely
normal thing in Korea I was really
shocked and she was like like yeah it
it's happened to me personally and it's
also happened to a lot of people that I
know so yeah um in my experience and
after speaking to some of my Korean
friends they also agree that making and
keeping Friends of the opposite gender
here is just difficult a lot of the time
I want to end by saying that making
friendships abroad is definitely
challenging but it's also one of the
most rewarding things I've ever done
done I have met some absolutely amazing
people here in Korea and definitely made
friends for life here and I don't know I
just think there's something so special
about making friends with people from
other countries I just think it's
amazing that you can make friends with
people from different cultures different
backgrounds it just allows you to
connect with that culture on a deeper
level and it also just allows you to
broaden your perspective on a lot of
things living a abroad has really made
me value good quality friendships a lot
more than it I had previously just
living in the UK when you're living
abroad honestly your friends are your
family and so choosing the right people
is really important I wish you the
absolute best of luck when making
friends abroad I hope that you meet some
amazing people who add value to your
life not like the people that I
mentioned in episode one and please
remember to not let the need for human
connection especially the need for human
connection when you're in a foreign
country to Cloud your judgment on who
you allow into your life I'm traveling
to husan this weekend and I will be
vlogging it so that will probably be in
next week's video so hopefully I will
see you then thank you so much for
watching and hopefully I will see you in
my next video bye guys
[Music]
